text:
Understanding Alzheimer's Disease: A Journey into the Mind's Maze
Artur Moura
Capstone sprint 1

Alzheimer disease is a progressive, irreversible brain disorder
1 in 3 seniors dies with Alzheimer's or another dementia. It kills more than breast cancer and prostate cancer combined.

Early detection with machine learning could lead to better management and improved patient outcomes
Only 1 in 4 people with dementia receive a diagnosis. Lack of diagnosis prevents access to treatment and care
Reduce healthcare costs through earlier identification rather than later treatment

If Alzheimer was a country
It would be the 18th largest economy in the world
At $1 trillion per year, dementia costs rival major economies like Canada and Indonesia

Data Introduction
MRI scans of Alzheimer's and healthy patients
Images cover cross-sections of the brain and show structural differences
Non demented
Very early demented
Moderately demented
Mild demented

NEXT STEPS
Preprocess images - rescale, make consistent sizes, normalize pixel values
Extract image features through convolutional layers of deep learning model
Train and validate model on dataset with evaluation metrics

The dataset used for this project was from the OASIS dataset created by Dr. Randy Buckner at the Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI) at Harvard University in collaboration with the Washington University Alzheimer's Disease Research Center.
https://www.oasis-brains.org/#dataAccess
